segments.text: Job began to speak again.
I promise you—as God lives, who has denied me justice, the Almighty, who has made my life bitter,
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: for as long as I have life, while the breath of God remains in my nostrils—
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: my lips will never speak lies, my tongue will never be dishonest.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: I will never agree that you are right; I will insist on my innocence until the day I die.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: I'm convinced I'm right and will never give up believing this; my conscience will not condemn me as long as I live.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: May my enemy become like the wicked; may those who oppose me become like those who do evil.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: For what hope do those who reject God have when he cuts them down, when God brings their lives to an end?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: When times of trouble come to them, will God hear their cry for help?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Do they have a good relationship with the Almighty? Can they call on God at any time?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Let me explain God's power to you. I will not keep anything back that the Almighty has planned.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: If all of you have recognized this, why do you talk such vain nonsense?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: This is what God provides as the destiny for the wicked, this is the inheritance that the ruthless will receive from the Almighty—
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: even if they have many children, they will experience violent deaths or die of starvation.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Those who survive will die from disease, and even their widows will not mourn for them.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Even though they pile up silver like dust, and clothes like heaps of clay,
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: those who do good will wear the clothes and the innocent will divide the silver among them.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: They build their houses like a moth;  like some flimsy shelter made by a watchman.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: They go to bed rich, but never again! For when they wake up, it's all gone.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Waves of panic flood over them; in the night a whirlwind snatches them away.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: The east wind picks them up and they're gone, carried away from where they were.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: The wind blows at them with full force; they try desperately to escape.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: People clap their hands at them and hiss at them wherever they are.
segments.speaker: Unknown
